text.title: Acceptance Criteria Quality Rules
text.summary: Testability rules, story-type guidelines, and common mistakes with their consequences.

rule.statement: Every acceptance criterion must be independently testable: there must exist a concrete action that produces a verifiable, observable outcome. Reject any criterion that is subjective ("user-friendly"), implementation-specific ("store in MySQL"), or unmeasurable ("fast").
rule.rationale: Untestable criteria cannot be included in the Definition of Done; they become subjective acceptance disputes at delivery time.

rule.statement: Performance criteria must specify exact numeric thresholds: "Response time under 2 seconds at 95th percentile under 500 concurrent users" — not "the system must be fast".
rule.rationale: Without a numeric threshold, QA cannot write a load test and "fast" means different things to every stakeholder.

rule.statement: Security criteria must specify the role and the prohibited or required action: "Only users with Admin role can delete customer records." Never "the system must be secure."
rule.rationale: Role-based security criteria map directly to authorization test cases; vague security statements map to nothing testable.

section.title: Story Type Guidelines
section.p: Feature stories: focus on user outcomes, cover happy path and at least 2 alternative scenarios, include error handling, add performance criteria only if the feature has a response-time requirement.
Bug fixes: describe expected vs. actual behavior precisely, include reproduction steps, define the "fixed" state as an observable outcome.
Technical stories: focus on technical outcomes (throughput, error rate, API contract), include a measurable verification method, define "done" as a observable system state or metric.

antipattern.description: Too vague: "User can log in" — no context, no error handling, no boundary conditions.
antipattern.reason: Vague criteria cannot produce test cases; every meaningful word in a criterion must be independently testable.

antipattern.description: Implementation details: "Store session token in Redis with 30-minute TTL" — the how, not the what.
antipattern.reason: Implementation criteria couple the spec to a specific technical choice; when the implementation changes the criteria become false without the behavior changing.

antipattern.description: Only happy path: no error scenarios, no boundary conditions.
antipattern.reason: Error paths are discovered during UAT; correcting them after development costs 10x more than specifying them upfront.

antipattern.description: Too many criteria for one story: 50+ criteria indicate the story should be split.
antipattern.reason: A story with 50 criteria takes a sprint to test; split into smaller stories that can be independently accepted and deployed.
